paper_title: Absorbed dose calculation for a realistic CT-derived mouse phantom irradiated with a standard Cs-137 cell irradiator using a Monte Carlo method

methods.description: Experimental procedure using a PMMA phantom and Gafchromic EBT3 film to measure the dose profile from a standard Cs-137 cell irradiator, replicating a mouse soft tissue xenograft model setup.

steps.description: A PMMA phantom, manufactured by our research group [50], was used for dose measurement. The phantom was made of a PMMA slab with dimensions of (6.75 × 2.25 × 1.50) cm 3 and an extrusion with diameter of (0.50 × 0.50 × 0.75) cm 3 was implanted on the side to represent the radiological characteristics and approximate shape of a mouse soft tissue with a xenograft tumour model in the flank as demonstrated in Fig 3A.
steps.searchable_summary: Fabricate a PMMA phantom with specific dimensions and an extrusion representing a tumour model.

steps.description: The phantom was sliced into two equivalent slabs to allow for the secure placement of radiochromic film.
steps.searchable_summary: Slice the PMMA phantom into two equivalent slabs.

steps.description: A piece of Gafchromic EBT3 film (Ashland Inc, Covington, Kentucky, USA) was first placed on the handmade polystyrene in the collimator and was irradiated.
steps.searchable_summary: Place Gafchromic EBT3 film on polystyrene support within the collimator and irradiate it.

steps.description: Then the corresponding field was marked on the polystyrene support to indicate the collimated field.
steps.searchable_summary: Mark the collimated field on the polystyrene support based on the irradiated film.
outputs: Polystyrene support with marked collimated field

steps.description: A rectangular piece of Gafchromic EBT3 film was sandwiched between the slabs and the phantom was placed inside the collimator, positioning the sandwiched Gafchromic EBT3 film right at the midplane of the collimator.
steps.searchable_summary: Sandwich a rectangular piece of Gafchromic EBT3 film between the PMMA slabs and position the assembled phantom with film at the collimator midplane.
outputs: Assembled PMMA phantom with sandwiched film positioned in collimator

steps.description: The tumour model was accurately positioned within the indicated collimated beam and the PMMA phantom was irradiated to obtain dose profile measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: Accurately position the tumour model within the marked collimated beam and irradiate the PMMA phantom.
inputs: Assembled PMMA phantom positioned in collimator
outputs: Irradiated Gafchromic EBT3 film (for dose measurement)

steps.description: Film dosimetry was performed based on the Gafchromic film dosimetry procedure given in previous studies [50,55], and a dose profile measurement was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Gafchromic film dosimetry analysis on the irradiated film to obtain a dose profile measurement.
inputs: Irradiated Gafchromic EBT3 film (for dose measurement)

methods.description: Monte Carlo simulation using EGSnrc/DOSXYZnrc to calculate the dose distribution within the PMMA phantom setup, replicating the experimental irradiation conditions.

steps.description: MC modelling of the PMMA phantom irradiation was performed and phantom doses were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up and run Monte Carlo simulation for PMMA phantom irradiation to calculate doses.

steps.description: Material-specific data files for the PMMA phantom were created using EGSnrc PEGS4 [52].
steps.searchable_summary: Create material data files for PMMA using EGSnrc PEGS4.

steps.description: The experimental irradiation set-up of the PMMA phantom (which was described in the previous section) was replicated using DOSXYZnrc (a user code for EGSnrc) [56].
steps.searchable_summary: Replicate the experimental PMMA phantom irradiation setup in DOSXYZnrc.
outputs: DOSXYZnrc simulation geometry and source definition

steps.description: The voxels for the PMMA phantom and outside the phantom were set to PMMA521ICRU and ICRUAIR521 (material name for PMMA and air in EGSnrc).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign material properties (PMMA, air) to voxels in the DOSXYZnrc geometry.

steps.description: The phase-space source (as described in section 2.2.2) was positioned at 0 and 180 degrees to the phantom geometry the particles transported through the voxelized phantom.
steps.searchable_summary: Position the phase-space source relative to the phantom geometry and simulate particle transport.

steps.description: The tumour model centre point was set as the isocentre for phase-space file and dose deposited in each voxel was calculated and written to the '.3DDOSE' file (default output file of DOSXYZnrc code).
steps.searchable_summary: Set the tumour model center as the isocenter, calculate dose deposited in each voxel, and write results to a .3DDOSE file.

steps.description: The interactions explained in section 2.2.2 were also included in DOSXYZnrc simulations [56].
steps.searchable_summary: Include specified radiation interaction physics in the DOSXYZnrc simulation.

steps.description: 5 × 10 9 gamma-ray photon histories were used for dose calculation [47].
steps.searchable_summary: Run the simulation using 5 x 10^9 gamma-ray photon histories.

methods.method_name: Dose Distribution Visualization and DVH Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of calculated dose distributions from Monte Carlo simulations through visual review and generation of Dose-Volume Histograms (DVHs) using the CERR toolkit.

steps.description: To visualize the calculated doses on the image set, both the CT-image set and 3DDOSE files were loaded into CERR and coronal and sagittal isodose lines and dose colourwash were displayed.
steps.searchable_summary: Load CT image set and 3DDOSE file into CERR and display coronal/sagittal isodose lines and dose colourwash.
outputs: Visual display of dose distribution (isodose lines, colourwash)

steps.description: The tumour, heart, liver, Llung (left lung), Rlung (right lung), Lkidney (left kidney), Rkidney (right kidney), and spinal column were contoured on each slice of the CT-image set for DVHs generation.
steps.searchable_summary: Contour specified organs-at-risk (OARs) and the tumour on each slice of the CT image set.

steps.description: To perform an accurate contouring for the tumour and all OARs, a whole-body mouse ATLAS from coregistered x-ray CT data of a normal mouse was used as guideline [61].
steps.searchable_summary: Use a whole-body mouse ATLAS as a guideline for accurate contouring.

steps.description: To convert the calculated MC doses to absolute dose for DVH analysis, the calibration method proposed by Popescu et al [62] was employed.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert calculated Monte Carlo doses to absolute doses using the calibration method by Popescu et al [62].

steps.description: Then the DVHs were plotted for 20 Gy absolute prescribed dose.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and plot Dose-Volume Histograms (DVHs) for contoured structures based on absolute dose distribution and a 20 Gy prescribed dose.
